understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to use a limited number of steps to maneuver selectable pieces around a 3-pixels-per-cell grid to reach goal tile(s) (marked as +), likely by positioning at least one selectable piece onto a goal location while respecting obstacles.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the currently selected piece exactly one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, updating position by ±3 on x or y when successful. If an adjacent object (wall, block, boundary, or another piece) was in the movement direction, the move could be blocked (no position change), as seen when moving UP from [15, 21] and moving RIGHT from [21, 21]. Some adjacent objects may be pushable blocks: if a pushable is immediately next to the selected piece in the movement direction and the space beyond is available, both would shift; otherwise the move would fail. CLICK allowed switching control to a different selectable piece by specifying its exact pixel coordinate (e.g., switching between pieces at [12, 21], [15, 21], and [21, 21]); CLICK did not consume movement distance but did consume a step. Walls/boundaries acted as blockers in at least some directions (e.g., right of [21, 21]), and the movement_blocked hint would indicate adjacency but not guarantee failure.
